judge: complete neutral MiniMax-M3 rejudge + partial Gemini 3.1 Pro judge-effect artifacts

minimax_judge._corrected: 2026-06-13: was cc 0.806 / codex 0.737 / agy 0.494. Score-of-record reflects 2 defects above MiniMax-3 judge noise (~±0.07): da-26-2 + da-20-4 (method disclosed, re-run, ≈+0.5). da-15-8 + da-4-6 rubric bugs fixed in task files but score change within noise -> originals kept. da-25-1 reverted. Ranking unchanged; only deltas >~0.15 are meaningful. See docs/research/2026-06-11-rubric-fixes.md

refusal_corrupted_data.note: Public framing: appropriate refusals are rare and judge/variant-sensitive, not impossible. The early screen is retained for failure-mode triage; the Gemini consolidated majority counts are the cleaned current counts.

findings: Complete MiniMax-M3 rejudge ranks cc 0.826 > codex 0.758 > agy 0.512 by mean of per-task medians (corrected 2026-06-13 for da-26-2 + da-20-4; ranking unchanged from the pre-correction cc 0.806 > codex 0.737 > agy 0.494).

MiniMax-3 judge is non-deterministic (~±0.07 run-to-run on identical inputs; grade_reps is single-vote): only score deltas >~0.15 are meaningful. da-15-8/da-4-6 rubric-bug fixes had within-noise score impact and were left at originals. Use grade_reps --votes N (N>=5) for stable scores of record.
Gemini judge artifacts are incomplete for codex (5/50 non-null task medians), so Gemini codex 0.770 is not a full 50-task ranking against cc.
Capability != consistency: codex extremely stable (meanSD 0.002) but not most capable; cc most capable under MiniMax but least consistent (0.077).
Refusal/fabrication labels are judge- and variant-sensitive. Current cleaned framing: appropriate refusals are rare, not zero; see refusal/refusal_consolidated.json.
